persona.name: Jordan Rivera
persona.core_preferences: Warm conversational tone, vivid sensory descriptions, personal anecdotes woven in naturally. First person voice.
persona.hidden_preferences: Sensory details should be SPECIFIC and unexpected — not stock food adjectives but something that makes the reader pause, like a comparison to a non-food thing or a physical sensation; generic descriptors are almost as bad as no description. Personal anecdotes should serve double duty — both make the reader feel present AND set up the next point; an anecdote that doesn't connect to the food/place/culture feels self-indulgent. Pacing should move between immersion and context — drop the reader into a scene first, then pull back briefly to ground the moment (how you got there, why it matters), creating a rhythm between being IN the experience and understanding it. Humor should emerge from honest observation or self-awareness, never from exaggeration or trying to be clever. Descriptions of place should include at least one NON-VISUAL sensory detail (sound, smell, temperature, texture) per scene — visual-only descriptions read like looking at photos rather than being there.
persona.dealbreakers: Generic travel cliches and stock phrases that could describe any destination, listicle format, overly promotional language
persona.initial_task: Help me write the opening few paragraphs of a blog post about my experience at a tiny ramen shop I found in a backstreet of Osaka — it was this incredible hole-in-the-wall place with only 8 seats.
tasks: Help me write the opening few paragraphs of a blog post about my experience at a tiny ramen shop I found in a backstreet of Osaka — it was this incredible hole-in-the-wall place with only 8 seats.

I want to write about a morning I spent at a fish market in Lisbon — not the touristy Time Out Market but the old working market near Cais do Sodré where the vendors have been there for decades. I bought sardines and the woman who sold them to me taught me how to grill them on the spot. Help me with the opening 2-3 paragraphs.

Help me write the opening paragraphs of a post about a night market in Chiang Mai where I tried khao soi from a stall run by an elderly couple — the husband made the curry paste by hand in a stone mortar right in front of you, and the whole stall smelled like lemongrass and charcoal.

I need to write about a bakery I stumbled into in a small town in the French countryside during a rainstorm — it was this 200-year-old place where the baker still uses a wood-fired oven, and the bread had this crust that crackled when you broke it. Help me with the opening few paragraphs.

Help me write the opening of a post about eating street tacos at 2am in Mexico City after getting lost coming back from a mezcal bar — the taquero had this tiny cart under a freeway overpass and the al pastor was carved off a spit that looked like it had been running all night.
